body.goal:
Phase 5 exposes Vault Graph to agents through MCP. The server should make the existing search, graph, decision-trace, status, and context-pack services easy for Codex, Claude, Cursor, OpenCode, and other agents to consume without asking them to scan an entire Vault.
The deliverable is not a new reasoning layer, not an answer-generation service, not memory projections, not remote hosting, and not durable Vault publication. The deliverable is a local, read-only MCP adapter over already-defined Vault Graph application services.
MCP output remains working context. If a tool response or prompt result suggests durable knowledge, that follow-up must go through Vault's normal source capture, valida...

supportingSections.heading: Purpose
supportingSections.content:
Phase 5 exposes Vault Graph to agents through MCP. The server should make the existing search, graph, decision-trace, status, and context-pack services easy for Codex, Claude, Cursor, OpenCode, and other agents to consume without asking them to scan an entire Vault.
The deliverable is not a new reasoning layer, not an answer-generation service, not memory projections, not remote hosting, and not durable Vault publication. The deliverable is a local, read-only MCP adapter over already-defined Vault Graph application services.
MCP output remains working context. If a tool response or prompt result suggests durable knowledge, that follow-up must go through Vault's normal source capture, validation, release gate, and Git history workflow.
items: Review the Purpose section in the source Markdown.

supportingSections.heading: Phase Slices
supportingSections.content:
| Slice | Change | User Value | Explicitly Not Included | | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Phase 5A | Add local stdio MCP server foundation, vg serve --mcp, service factory, capability registration, and Codex-compatible config examples | agents can connect to Vault Graph without a custom CLI wrapper | Streamable HTTP, auth, remote hosting, resources beyond capability skeleton, answer synthesis |
| Phase 5B | Add read-only MCP resources and resource templates over indexed Vault content, graph entities, current context, and generated context packs | agents can request precise evidence resources instead of whole-Vault scans | Vault writes, resource subscriptions, durable pack store, full document listing by default |
| Phase 5C | Add service-backed MCP tools and prompts over existing retrieval, graph, decision-trace, status, and context-pack services | agents can search, build context packs, inspect graph evidence, and use repeatable workflows | ask_vault, Phase 6 memory tools, LLM clients, autonomous wiki updates |
The slices separate transport, resources, and tool/prompt behavior. This keeps the MCP adapter thin and prevents agent-facing integration from becoming a second application la...

supportingSections.heading: Current Phase 5 Positions
supportingSections.content: Default transport is stdio. It is the simplest local agent integration and avoids network-origin and authentication questions. Streamable HTTP is deferred to the optional HTTP serving phase. It needs origin validation, localhost binding policy, authentication, and session hardening before it can be safe. Use the official Python MCP SDK at implementation time, with a preference for its high-level server API where it preserves structured output and explicit resource/tool/prompt registration. MCP responses should return structured JSON as the contract. Text content is a compatibility mirror, not the source of truth. Only service-backed tools are registered. ask_vault, project-memory summaries, open-question tools, recent-change tools, and result-explanation tools remain future tools until their backing application services exist. Prompt templates may mention future workflows, but they must instruct agents to call only currently registered tools and to treat all Vault Graph output as working context. Context pack resources are generated artifacts. Until a durable pack store is designed, vault://context/packs/{pack_id} is backed by a bounded in-process cache and can be regenerated by c...

supportingSections.heading: Cross-Slice Invariants
supportingSections.content: Vault remains the durable source of truth. MCP is an adapter over application services. It must not query SQLite, Chroma, rustworkx, or Vault files directly when an application service exists. MCP tools, resources, and prompts must not write, rename, rewrite, delete, or publish Vault content. Phase 5 read paths use read-only store construction. They must not initialize missing indexes, create caches, or run indexing as a side effect. Evidence chunks remain the authority unit for search and context-pack output: (vault_id, document_id, chunk_id). Tool and resource output must preserve Vault IDs where collision is possible. Warnings are first-class structured fields and must not be hidden by text mirrors or prompts. Multi-vault scope is explicit. Default MCP calls use the active Vault only. Graph and cross-Vault graph expansion are opt-in, matching Phase 3 and Phase 4 behavior. The MCP server must write logs to stderr, never stdout, while using stdio. stdout is reserved for MCP protocol messages.
items: Vault remains the durable source of truth.
MCP is an adapter over application services. It must not query SQLite, Chroma,
MCP tools, resources, and prompts must not write, rename, rewrite, delete, or
Phase 5 read paths use read-only store construction. They must not initialize
Evidence chunks remain the authority unit for search and context-pack output:
Tool and resource output must preserve Vault IDs where collision is possible.
Warnings are first-class structured fields and must not be hidden by text
Multi-vault scope is explicit. Default MCP calls use the active Vault only.
Graph and cross-Vault graph expansion are opt-in, matching Phase 3 and Phase 4
The MCP server must write logs to stderr, never stdout, while using stdio.

codeBlocks.content:
vg serve --mcp --state PATH
  -> load VaultCatalog
  -> open read-only application services
  -> create MCP server
  -> register capabilities
  -> run stdio transport

resources/read URI
  -> parse and validate vault:// URI
  -> resolve Vault ID through VaultCatalog
  -> resolve indexed document/chunk/entity evidence through stores/services
  -> return text or JSON resource content with evidence metadata and warnings

tools/call
  -> validate JSON arguments
  -> convert scope input into QueryScope
  -> call the matching application service
  -> return structuredContent plus text mirror
  -> include warnings, revisions, backend use, and recovery hints

supportingSections.heading: Error And Degradation Policy
supportingSections.content: Invalid MCP arguments, invalid scope, unknown Vault IDs, unsupported resource URIs, and missing required fields are protocol-level invalid-parameter errors. Missing indexes, unhealthy backends, stale projections, and unavailable graph state become structured tool/resource errors or warnings depending on the underlying service contract. Search and context tools may degrade from vector to keyword when existing retrieval behavior permits it, but the degraded state must be visible. Graph tools fail or degrade only when graph was explicitly requested. Resource reads for missing generated context packs return a not-found error with guidance to rerun build_context_pack. MCP prompts must never instruct agents to treat generated output as durable truth or to update Vault files directly.
items: Invalid MCP arguments, invalid scope, unknown Vault IDs, unsupported resource
Missing indexes, unhealthy backends, stale projections, and unavailable graph

Graph tools fail or degrade only when graph was explicitly requested.

supportingSections.heading: Multi-Vault Policy
supportingSections.content: scope=None uses the active Vault. scope.vault_ids selects explicit Vault IDs. scope.all_vaults=true expands to all enabled Vault IDs. include_cross_vault=true is valid only when graph behavior is explicit and the selected scope has more than one Vault. Resource URIs always include a Vault ID except generated context pack URIs. Identical paths, titles, chunk IDs, entity labels, or warning codes from different Vaults must not collide in MCP output.

scope.vault_ids selects explicit Vault IDs.
scope.all_vaults=true expands to all enabled Vault IDs.

supportingSections.heading: Handoff
supportingSections.content:
Phase 5 implementation planning should proceed in this order:
Phase 5A: dependency choice, vault_graph.mcp package, stdio server, service factory, vg serve --mcp, error mapping, import boundaries, and local config examples. Phase 5B: URI parser, resource templates, metadata-backed resources, graph-entity resource, bounded context-pack cache, and resource tests. Phase 5C: service-backed tools, prompt templates, structured output mirrors, tool/prompt tests, and agent workflow smoke tests.
Each slice should preserve the rule that Vault Graph writes only derived state and never edits Vault content.
items: Phase 5A: dependency choice, vault_graph.mcp package, stdio server, service
Phase 5B: URI parser, resource templates, metadata-backed resources,
Phase 5C: service-backed tools, prompt templates, structured output mirrors,

supportingSections.heading: External Protocol Notes
supportingSections.content:
The Phase 5 design follows the MCP 2025-06-18 specification:
resources are application-driven context identified by URIs tools are model-invoked operations with JSON schemas and structured output prompts are user-controlled templates stdio servers must emit only MCP JSON-RPC messages on stdout Streamable HTTP requires origin validation, localhost binding care, and authentication before it is suitable for local Vault access

tools are model-invoked operations with JSON schemas and structured output
prompts are user-controlled templates
